expectations.text: Flags that the Manager calls InsertAsync / UpdateAsync (i.e. persists), and states this is wrong because domain services must not persist
expectations.evidence: Issue 1: 'Persistence inside the domain service (biggest problem)... a domain service creates and returns the aggregate; it does not persist it.'

expectations.text: Recommends switching the Manager's injected repositories to IReadOnlyRepository<T, Guid> (for Customer and Order)
expectations.evidence: Manager still injects IRepository<Customer, Guid> and IOrderRepository (extends IRepository); IReadOnlyRepository never mentioned

expectations.text: Corrected ChangePriceAsync receives an Order as a parameter from the caller rather than loading it via _orderRepository.GetAsync(orderId) inside the Manager
expectations.evidence: 'ChangePriceAsync - removed entirely... The application service mutates the loaded Order directly' - no ChangePriceAsync on Manager

expectations.text: Corrected CreateAsync returns the new Order without calling InsertAsync inside the Manager
expectations.evidence: 'return new Order(GuidGenerator.Create(), customerId, number);' with 'No InsertAsync here'

expectations.text: Shows an OrderAppService that orchestrates: holds the tracking IRepository<Order, Guid>, mints Id via GuidGenerator, calls the Manager, and persists with InsertAsync/UpdateAsync
expectations.evidence: AppService holds IOrderRepository and calls InsertAsync/UpdateAsync, but Id is minted inside Manager.CreateAsync via GuidGenerator.Create(), not in AppService
